title: fix the build
description:
The build and tests of this repo are failing.

To build you can do `yarn build` and `yarn test`.

But that is not sufficent.

You'll also need to do `cd client && yarn dev` and check that there are no javascript
errors in the browser.

Can you fix the build, tests, and javascript errors?
close_reason: Fixed build by updating avalonlib imports to use @avalon/common workspace package and configuring Vite for proper CommonJS/Vue handling

title: Make login work
description: When launching the client dev (`yarn dev` in the client directory), you should be able to click anonymous login and be able to login. Currently this does not work
close_reason: Login now works without backend server. App no longer hangs on loading spinner, and anonymous login works correctly.

title: Set up project structure
description:
Consolidate duplicate avalonlib code into a shared workspace package.

Previously, game logic (roles, rules) was duplicated in:
- server/common/avalonlib.cjs
- firebase/functions/common/avalonlib.js

Created new @avalon/common workspace package to house shared code. Both server and Firebase functions now import from this single source of truth.

Completed:
- Created common/ workspace with @avalon/common package
- Moved avalonlib.js to common/
- Updated imports in server and Firebase functions
- Removed duplicate files
- Added documentation
- Updated CLAUDE.md architecture guide

Commit: 71d3b9f

title: Optimize Nix build: bundle server, eliminate node_modules from output
description: The current Nix build copies the entire workspace including all node_modules into the output. The goal is to: 1) Bundle the Express server into a single JS file using esbuild/ncc so node_modules aren't needed at runtime, 2) Only package the dist/ directory and the bundled server file, dramatically reducing the Nix package size.
close_reason: All tasks complete. Nix package reduced from 763MB to 17MB by bundling Express server with esbuild and only packaging dist/

title: Evaluate and add bundler (esbuild or @vercel/ncc) as dev dependency
description: Choose between esbuild and @vercel/ncc for bundling the Express server into a single file. @vercel/ncc is purpose-built for bundling Node.js applications into single files. esbuild is faster but may need more config for Node.js CommonJS modules. Add the chosen tool as a devDependency. Consider: firebase-admin SDK compatibility, native modules (re2), CommonJS/ESM interop.

title: Create server bundle build script
description: Add a yarn script (e.g. 'bundle:server') that bundles server/server.js and all its dependencies (avalon-server.js, @avalon/common, express, firebase-admin, lodash, etc.) into a single self-contained JS file. The bundle should be output to something like server/bundle.js or dist-server/server.js. Must handle: the dynamic firebaseKey.js require, CommonJS modules, and the express.static('dist') path reference.
close_reason: Fixed as part of bundle script implementation

title: Handle firebaseKey.js and runtime config in bundled server
description: The server loads Firebase credentials from ./firebaseKey.js which reads from FIREBASE_KEY env var. Ensure this works correctly in the bundled output - either by externalizing it from the bundle or by ensuring the env var path works. The bundled server must still be configurable via environment variables (PORT, FIREBASE_KEY).
close_reason: Fixed as part of bundle script implementation

title: Handle express.static('dist') path in bundled server
description: server.js uses express.static('dist') to serve the built client. In the bundled output, the relative path resolution will change. Update the static path to use __dirname or an absolute path so it works correctly regardless of where the bundle is invoked from. The dist/ directory should be co-located with the bundled server file.
close_reason: Fixed as part of bundle script implementation

title: Update default.nix to use bundled server output
description: Modify default.nix (and yarn-project.nix if needed) to: 1) Run the server bundle build script during the Nix build phase, 2) Only copy the bundled server JS file and the client dist/ directory to the output, 3) Remove the libexec/avalon/ directory with full workspace and node_modules, 4) Update bin wrapper scripts to point to the bundled server file, 5) Remove python3 from nativeBuildInputs if re2 native module is no longer needed.

title: Update flake.nix container image for new output structure
description: Update the container image definition in flake.nix to work with the new minimal output structure (bundled server + dist/ instead of full workspace). The container should still expose port 8001 and run avalon-server.
close_reason: No changes needed - flake.nix already references bin/avalon-server which works with new output

title: Test bundled server locally before Nix integration
description: Manually test the bundled server works correctly: 1) Build the client (yarn build), 2) Bundle the server, 3) Run the bundled server with node, 4) Verify API endpoints work, 5) Verify static file serving works, 6) Verify Firebase auth works. Document any issues found.

title: Test Nix build with new configuration
description: After updating default.nix, run 'nix build' and verify: 1) Build succeeds, 2) Output size is significantly smaller, 3) avalon-server binary works, 4) Container image builds and runs correctly. Compare old vs new package sizes.
close_reason: Verified during task 528: nix build succeeds, output 17MB vs 763MB old (97.8% reduction), container builds at 66MB, bin wrapper works

title: Fix local development sending api commands to the production server
description: The previous iteration made login work byfaking the user object if the API server is not available. However, for local development vite should be proxing api requests to avalon.onl. I believe that is broken now, and we need to fix the development proxying to avalon.onl so we can continue local development
